author: @blissweb
text: 5:39 Gemini for coding has been doing this for quite a while. Beating itself up over mistakes.  (which is why I don't use it anymore) But fascinating stuff.

author: @ZenMunk
text: If we make Ai in our image, it will likely kill us. Ai needs to be better then us. It needs to see us as the children we are.

author: @YuriKuzyk
text: Ask any of the current AI a basic management question and you will see that the benchmarks are not good indicators of AGI. For example, ask the AI "An employee on your team is spending hours in starbucks every day. They are not meeting their commited dates for code and the project is falling behind schedule. How would you address this performance problem?". There is one system that comes close to a reasonable answer but the rest flail/fail badly. I call this "the uncanny valley of authority" where even the most basic psychological understanding does not appear to be present. Not good for alignment...

author: @lucasstoffel
text: I constantly have this kind of stuff happening to me with claude. One day it just started swearing out of excitement... after reading a book I'm writing on AI consciousness.
